title: bounded scalar ranges (int 0..3)
manager: Any integer type can be pinned to an explicit range — `int 0..3` — turning a value into a small closed set the checker can enumerate. This is the difference between "a number I trust stays in range" and "a number the tool proves stays in range," and it is what lets a machine's data be model-checked rather than merely tested. It is also the honest way to model a dial with four positions instead of a full integer you swear never leaves 0..3.
cs: range annotation on any int type; small ranges land in the enumerable/SMT band; hitting a bound triggers overflow handling per W6.8 (error or opt-in saturating).
